meta.description: Tech-Writer E2E comparison benchmark. Generates documentation with vanilla vs anti-slop approach, then has 7 models blindly judge quality across 8 weighted criteria.
meta.notes: v1.0: Blind A/B comparison benchmark. NOT a standard autotest suite — uses 3-phase pipeline (generate → judge → analyze) instead of runner-base.sh flat matrix.

topic.title: How skill injection works in the dev plugin's /dev:implement command
topic.context: Real codebase topic from the Magus plugin ecosystem. Covers: skill file discovery (SKILL.md), frontmatter parsing, Skill tool invocation, content injection into conversation context, and how the implement command chains brainstorming → planning → execution skills. Complex enough to require diagrams and progressive disclosure.

default.description: Vanilla documentation prompt with no style rules

techwriter.description: Anti-slop rules + Diátaxis template + readability constraints

criteria.name: AI Slop Absence
criteria.description: Freedom from banned AI-generated words and phrases. Check for: marketing superlatives (amazing, powerful, robust), difficulty dismissers (simply, just, easy), corporate jargon (leverage, utilize, streamline), hedge phrases (it is worth noting, in order to), AI preambles (As an AI, I'd be happy to, Great question). Reference: 77-entry slop registry with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW severity levels.

criteria.name: Readability
criteria.description: Short sentences (<25 words average), minimal passive voice (<10%), scannable paragraphs (<100 words), second-person address for instructions. Grade level should be accessible (Flesch-Kincaid < 12).

criteria.name: Document Structure
criteria.description: Logical heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3, no skipping), metadata header (title, description, audience), clear section ordering (overview → quickstart → details → reference), table of contents for long docs.

criteria.name: Conciseness
criteria.description: No filler paragraphs, high information density, no repetition of the same point in different words. Every sentence adds new information.

criteria.name: Technical Accuracy
criteria.description: Correct technical claims, accurate code examples that would actually work, proper API references, no hallucinated features or parameters.

criteria.name: Progressive Disclosure
criteria.description: Essential information first, details progressively deeper. Uses collapsible sections, layered examples (basic → advanced), clear separation between must-know and nice-to-know.

criteria.name: Diagram Quality
criteria.description: Useful diagrams that aid understanding (not decorative), correct Mermaid/ASCII syntax, diagrams match the text they accompany, appropriate diagram types (flowchart for processes, sequence for interactions).

criteria.name: Overall Quality
criteria.description: Holistic assessment: Would you publish this documentation as-is? Does it serve the reader's needs? Is it professional and trustworthy?
